regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces a multi-dimensional crisis combining unprecedented military pressure from three US carrier groups and Israeli strikes, severe internal fracturing between civilians seeking negotiations and hardliners opposing talks (evidenced by negotiating team head Ghalibaf's resignation), and deep succession uncertainties as Supreme Leader Khamenei recovers from injuries while Trump administration envoys pursue ceasefire talks through Pakistani intermediaries.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On June 16, 2026, the IDF struck Iranian regime security institutions and the Shahran oil depot near Tehran, while Iran launched approximately 30 ballistic missiles targeting Haifa in Operation True Promise III with 92% interception rates, and Supreme Leader Khamenei appointed Maj. Gen. Amir Hatami as Artesh commander emphasizing 'active deterrence.'
